name: Perceive, Plan, Execute, Reflect, Iterate
description: Production delivery loop for the portable Digital Office multi-Agent system. It keeps Agent work observable, stateful, reviewable, and explicitly user-confirmed before iteration changes are applied.

research_foundations.name: ReAct
research_foundations.principle: Interleave reasoning, tool action, and observation; do not rely on one-shot answers for uncertain work.

research_foundations.name: Reflexion
research_foundations.principle: Turn failed or partial task outcomes into explicit reflective feedback for future trials.

research_foundations.name: Generative Agents
research_foundations.principle: Use memory, reflection, and planning as separate state layers instead of mixing all context into a single prompt.

research_foundations.name: Voyager
research_foundations.principle: Build reusable skills and knowledge through an explicit, reviewed library rather than silent self-modification.

research_foundations.name: MetaGPT
research_foundations.principle: Role-based SOPs and structured handoffs reduce multi-Agent drift.

research_foundations.name: AutoGen
research_foundations.principle: Multi-Agent conversation should have explicit roles, boundaries, and human-controlled stopping conditions.

research_foundations.name: LangGraph durable execution
research_foundations.principle: Long-running Agent workflows need persisted state, resumability, checkpoints, and human-in-the-loop control.

research_foundations.name: SWE-bench
research_foundations.principle: Production claims need reproducible tasks and deterministic judges.

research_foundations.name: RAGAS
research_foundations.principle: RAG quality should be judged by retrieval relevance, grounding, and answer faithfulness.

research_foundations.name: Self-RAG
research_foundations.principle: Retrieval should be critiqued before it is trusted as final context.

perceive.goal: Build the task context from explicit user intent, identity, permissions, project knowledge, company knowledge, licensed references, KeyMemory relay, router signals, and current system health.

plan.goal: Convert the perceived context into a role-based workflow plan with acceptance criteria, handoff contracts, risk, and deterministic checks.

execute.goal: Run the planned workflow through the router and registered Agents while capturing artifacts, observations, failures, retries, and gate results.

reflect.goal: Review what happened against the plan, evidence, user goal, quality gates, and known risks. Reflection produces decisions and proposals, not silent changes.

iterate.goal: Improve rules, workflows, Agent behavior, harness tasks, knowledge methods, or release packages only after the user can inspect the proposed change and explicitly confirm it.

hard_rules: Every production task should be represented as a loop run before it is described as completed.
Iteration is never automatic. The system may propose improvements, but user confirmation is required before application.
The proposal must explain what will change, why, expected impact, risk, rollback, affected Agents/workflows/knowledge, and required regression checks.
The secretary owns ambiguous routes, user confirmation, iteration proposals, and failed-gate recovery.
A production claim requires deterministic harness gates plus a reflection report.
